docs.title: UmaCore
docs.text: UmaCore is a Discord bot for tracking and managing Umamusume club member quotas. It handles daily fan-earning goals, bomb warnings, progress charts, and member management — all automatically.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Multi-club support with independent settings per club
  • Automated daily quota checks at configurable times
  • Bomb warning system (3-strike countdown for members falling behind)
  • Progress charts showing fan progression throughout the month
  • Member auto-detection (adds new members, deactivates those who leave)
  • Monthly reset handling
  • Discord account linking for personal DM notifications
  • Supports both Uma.moe API and ChronoGenesis scraping

docs.title: Navigation
docs.text:
• Getting Started — Invite the bot and get up and running
  • FAQ — Common questions and issues
  • Setup Guide — Self-hosting and configuration
  • Commands — All slash commands
  • Quota System — How quotas and bombs work
  • Data Sources — Uma.moe API and ChronoGenesis

docs.title: Data Sources
docs.text: UmaCore supports two data sources for fetching member fan counts.

docs.title: Uma.moe API (Recommended)
docs.text:
The primary data source. Fast and reliable.

Requires: circle_id set on the club (see below)

What it provides: - Full month history per member - Daily cumulative fan counts - Club ranking data

Finding your Circle ID: 1. Go to uma.moe/circles 2. Search for your club 3. Copy the numeric ID from the URL - Example: https://uma.moe/circles/860280110 → use 860280110 4. Set it with /add_club circle_id:860280110 or /edit_club circle_id:860280110

Timing notes: - Data updates around 15:10 UTC daily - On day 1 of the month, the bot fetches the previous month (current month hasn't populated yet) - If today's data isn't ready yet, the bot falls back to yesterday's data automatically

docs.title: ChronoGenesis Scraper (Fallback)
docs.text:
Used when a club has no circle_id, or when USE_UMAMOE_API=false is set in .env.

Requires: Chrome/Chromium installed on the host machine

Limitations: - Slower than the API - Dependent on ChronoGenesis page structure (may break if the site changes) - Less reliable overall

The bot automatically falls back to ChronoGenesis if the Uma.moe API fails.

docs.title: Switching Data Sources
docs.text:
To use Uma.moe API globally (default):

USE_UMAMOE_API=true

To force ChronoGenesis globally:

USE_UMAMOE_API=false

Per-club: if a club has no circle_id set, it always uses ChronoGenesis regardless of the global setting.

docs.title: Data Not Available Yet
docs.text: Uma.moe updates around 15:10 UTC each day. If the daily check runs before data is available, the bot automatically falls back to the previous day's data. It verifies the fallback isn't stale (by checking for fan growth) before using it — if it looks like yesterday's data hasn't changed, the check fails cleanly with an error rather than reporting incorrect numbers.

docs.title: Day 1 of the Month
docs.text: On the first day of a new month, the current month's data hasn't populated yet on Uma.moe. The bot automatically fetches the previous month's data as its primary source and uses the new month's day 0 endpoint to get accurate current totals. Reports on Day 1 are dated to the last day of the previous month.

docs.title: End-of-Month JST Rollover
docs.text: Uma.moe operates on JST. On the last day of the month, if the JST clock has rolled into the next month while UTC hasn't, rank data from the API may be empty or belong to the new period. The bot detects this and drops rank display from the report rather than showing incorrect values.

docs.title: Invalid or Missing Circle ID
docs.text: If a club's circle_id is missing or contains non-numeric characters, the bot sends an error embed to the report channel with instructions on how to find and set the correct ID. The daily check is skipped for that club until it's fixed.

docs.title: Lifetime vs Monthly Fan Counts
docs.text: The Uma.moe API returns lifetime cumulative fan counts. The bot converts these to monthly values automatically by detecting each member's first non-zero entry (their join day) and subtracting it. Members who joined before the current month start from 0 as expected.

docs.title: Cookie Consent Popup
docs.text: Some sessions trigger a cookie consent banner that can block the page from rendering. The bot attempts to click through it automatically, and falls back to removing it via JavaScript if clicking fails. If neither works, the scrape will fail and an error is reported.

docs.title: Page Load Failures
docs.text: If the chart container doesn't load within the timeout period, the bot saves a debug screenshot (debug_no_chart.png) and reports a scrape failure. This usually means the website is slow or down — retrying later via /force_check typically resolves it.

docs.title: Missing or Incomplete Table Data
docs.text: If a member's row has fewer columns than expected (e.g. they joined partway through the month and some day columns are empty), the bot uses 0 for those days rather than crashing. Members with a dash (-) for a day are also treated as 0 for that day.

docs.title: Retry Behaviour
docs.text: When a scrape fails, the bot retries up to 3 times with exponential backoff (10s → 20s → 40s). If all retries fail, an error embed is posted to the report channel explaining the most likely cause. No reports, bombs, or notifications are sent for that run.

docs.title: Concurrent Scrape Prevention
docs.text: The bot uses a per-club scrape lock to prevent two scrapes from running at the same time (e.g. a scheduled check overlapping with a /force_check). If a lock is already held, the second attempt is silently skipped. Locks automatically expire after 30 minutes to prevent deadlocks if the bot crashes mid-scrape.

docs.title: How do I set up the bot?
docs.text:
Use /add_club to register your club, then /set_report_channel to set where reports go. After that either wait for the automatic daily scrape or run /force_check to trigger it immediately.

See the Getting Started guide for the full walkthrough.

docs.title: How do I find my circle ID?
docs.text:
Uma.moe: Go to uma.moe/circles, search for your club, and copy the number from the URL. It looks something like 860280110.

ChronoGenesis: The ID is shown directly under your club name on the site. It looks something like 690001342.

docs.title: When does fan data update?
docs.text:
This happens once a day. The timing depends on which data source you're using:

  • ChronoGenesis — updates around 11:00 UTC
  • Uma.moe (used by the public bot) — updates around 16:00 UTC

It's recommended to set your scrape time to 17:00 UTC to ensure data is ready before the check runs.

docs.title: The bot didn't scrape at the time I set
docs.text: The bot won't run at the exact time you configured — it may be anywhere from 20 to 40 minutes late depending on how the internal scheduler lines up. This is expected behavior.

docs.title: How do I invite the bot to my server?
docs.text:
You can invite the public bot using the link below, or self-host it since the project is open source.

Invite UmaCore

docs.title: I have a problem
docs.text: Please describe your issue in detail in the #support channel on our Discord. Keep in mind that responses may not be instant.

docs.title: Invite the Bot
docs.text:
Click here to invite UmaCore to your server

Alternatively, you can self-host it — the bot is open source.

docs.title: First-Time Setup (Admin)
docs.text:
1. Add your club

/add_club club_name:YourClub scrape_url:... circle_id:860280110

See Finding Your Circle ID below if you're not sure what to put there.

2. Set your report and alert channels

/set_report_channel club:YourClub channel:#daily-reports
/set_alert_channel club:YourClub channel:#mod-alerts

3. Test it

Either wait for the automatic daily scrape, or trigger it manually:

/force_check club:YourClub

That's it — the bot will handle everything else automatically from here.

docs.title: Finding Your Circle ID
docs.text:
Uma.moe: Go to uma.moe/circles, search for your club, and copy the number from the URL. - Example: https://uma.moe/circles/860280110 → use 860280110

ChronoGenesis: The circle ID is shown directly under your club name on the site. It looks something like 690001342.

docs.title: For Members
docs.text:
Once your admin has set up the bot, you can link your Discord account to your trainer name to get personal DM notifications:

/link_trainer trainer_name:YourName club:YourClub

After linking you can use /my_status to check your own quota progress at any time.

docs.title: Privacy Policy
docs.text:
Last updated: April 1, 2026

This Privacy Policy describes what data UmaCore collects, how it is used, and how it is stored.

docs.title: 1. Data We Collect
docs.text:
UmaCore collects and stores only the minimum data required to function:

Data Purpose Discord Server ID Identify which server a configuration belongs to Discord Channel IDs Know where to send quota reports and notifications Discord User IDs Link Discord accounts to club members for DM notifications Club member names and fan counts Core quota tracking functionality Server configuration settings Store per-server preferences (quotas, schedules, thresholds)

We do not collect message content, passwords, payment information, or any other personal data.

docs.title: 2. How Data Is Used
docs.text:
Collected data is used exclusively to provide UmaCore's features:

  • Tracking daily fan-earning quotas per club
  • Sending bomb warnings and quota reports to configured channels
  • Delivering personal DM notifications to linked users
  • Generating progress charts

Data is never sold, shared with third parties, or used for advertising.

docs.title: 3. Data Storage
docs.text: Data is stored in a PostgreSQL database. Access is restricted to the bot and its operators. We take reasonable precautions to protect stored data, but cannot guarantee absolute security.

docs.title: 4. Data Retention
docs.text: Data is retained for as long as UmaCore is active in your server. If UmaCore is removed from a server, you may request deletion of your server's data by contacting us (see Section 6).

docs.title: 5. Your Rights
docs.text:
You may request:

  • A copy of the data stored for your server or Discord account
  • Deletion of your data

To make a request, contact us using the information in Section 6.

docs.title: 6. Contact
docs.text: For privacy-related requests or questions, open an issue on GitHub or contact the developer via Discord.

docs.title: 7. Changes to This Policy
docs.text: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. Continued use of UmaCore after changes are posted constitutes acceptance of the updated policy.

docs.title: How Quotas Work
docs.text: Each club has a quota — a fan-earning goal that members need to meet. The bot checks progress daily and compares each member's actual fans against the expected cumulative total.

docs.title: Deficit & Surplus
docs.text:
deficit_surplus = cumulative_fans - expected_fans
  • Positive → member is ahead of quota
  • Negative → member is behind quota
  • Surplus from previous days can cover future deficits

docs.title: Quota Periods
docs.text:
Period Description daily Quota applies per day (e.g. 1M/day = 30M/month) weekly Quota applies per 7 days (e.g. 5M/week) biweekly Quota applies per 14 days (e.g. 10M/2 weeks)

Set or change the period with /edit_club.

docs.title: Mid-Month Quota Changes
docs.text: You can change the quota at any time with /quota. The new quota applies from that day forward — historical data is unaffected and expected fans are recalculated automatically. The monthly info board also updates.

docs.title: Bomb System
docs.text: The bomb system warns members who consistently fall behind quota.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. A member falls behind quota for bomb_trigger_days consecutive days (default: 3)
  2. A bomb is activated — the member is notified via DM (if linked)
  3. The member has bomb_countdown_days days (default: 7) to get back on track
  4. If they catch up within the countdown, the bomb is defused
  5. If they don't, an alert is posted in the alert channel

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Configured per club via /edit_club:

Setting Default Description bomb_trigger_days 3 Days behind before bomb activates bomb_countdown_days 7 Days to recover before alert bombs_enabled true Toggle bomb system on/off

Use /bomb_status to see all active bombs for a club.

docs.title: Monthly Reset
docs.text:
At the start of each month, the bot automatically detects when fan counts drop significantly (below 50% of the previous total) and triggers a reset, which:

  • Clears quota history
  • Clears active bombs
  • Clears tracking data
  • Starts fresh for the new month

You can also trigger it manually with /reset_month if something went wrong.

docs.title: New Trainer Joins Mid-Month
docs.text:
When a trainer joins mid-month, the bot only knows their current total fan count — it has no data on how many fans they earned before joining. Because of this, their first day is recorded as +0 (no daily gain) and they are not held to any quota requirement for that day.

From the next day onward, the bot can calculate their actual daily gain and quota tracking begins normally.

This appears in the daily report embed as:

TrainerName    +0    [total fans]

No bomb strike is issued on the first day regardless of their deficit, since the baseline is being established.

docs.title: Trainer Leaves the Club
docs.text:
When a trainer no longer appears in the scraped data, the bot automatically deactivates them. Their quota history is preserved in the database but they are removed from all daily reports and their active bombs are cleared.

If they rejoin later in the same month, the bot reactivates them and treats it as a fresh join — the same first-day +0 behaviour applies, and their quota expectations are calculated from the return date, not the original join date.

docs.title: Manually Deactivated vs Auto-Deactivated
docs.text:
There are two kinds of deactivation:

  • Auto-deactivated — triggered when a trainer disappears from scrape data. They will be reactivated automatically if they reappear.
  • Manually deactivated — triggered via /deactivate_member. The bot will not reactivate them automatically, even if they show up in future scrapes. Use /activate_member to restore them.

docs.title: Bomb Countdown Only Decrements Once Per Day
docs.text: Even if the daily check runs more than once (e.g. after a /force_check), the bomb countdown only decrements once per calendar day. There is no risk of a bomb expiring faster than intended from repeated checks.

docs.title: Bomb Urgency Colours
docs.text:
The bomb emoji colour in the daily report reflects how much time is left:

Colour Days Remaining 🟡 Yellow 5 or more 🟠 Orange 3–4 🔴 Red 0–2

docs.title: Consecutive Days Resets Each Month
docs.text: The bomb trigger counts consecutive days behind quota within the current month only. If a member ends the previous month behind quota, that does not carry over — they start the new month with a clean streak count.

docs.title: Bombs Are Cleared on Monthly Reset
docs.text: When the bot detects a monthly reset, all active bombs for the club are deleted. Members must accumulate new consecutive behind-days in the new month before a bomb is re-issued.

docs.title: Bomb Alerts for Already-Removed Members
docs.text: If a member is manually deactivated while a bomb is still active, the bot will not send a kick alert for them. The bomb record is kept in the database for history but is otherwise ignored.

docs.title: Weekly / Biweekly Periods
docs.text:
When a club uses weekly or biweekly quota periods, the daily report shows progress within the current period rather than a running monthly total:

TrainerName    500K / 700K this week  (-200K overall)

The "overall" figure is the full month deficit/surplus. The per-period figure resets at the start of each new period.

docs.title: Multiple Quota Changes on the Same Day
docs.text: If /quota is run multiple times in a single day, the most recently set value takes effect. The monthly info board reflects the final value for that day.

docs.title: Automatic Reset Detection
docs.text: The bot detects a reset when a member's fan count drops to less than 50% of their previous recorded total. This handles the in-game monthly reset without any manual intervention. If a reset is incorrectly triggered, use /reset_month to force a clean state.

docs.title: No Previous Data (First Ever Scrape)
docs.text: On the very first scrape for a new club, there is no prior history to compare against. The bot skips reset detection entirely and adds all members as new. No reset is triggered even if fan counts appear low.

docs.title: DMs Disabled or User Not Linked
docs.text: If a member hasn't linked their Discord account via /link, or has Discord DMs disabled, bomb notifications and deficit alerts are simply not delivered to them. The report channel and alert channel still receive all notifications as normal — DM failures do not affect channel output.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Python 3.10+
  • PostgreSQL database (Neon, Supabase, or local)
  • Discord bot token
  • Chrome/Chromium — only needed if using ChronoGenesis scraper

docs.title: 1. Clone the repository
docs.text:
git clone <repository-url>
cd UmaCore

docs.title: 2. Install dependencies
docs.text: pip install -r requirements.txt

docs.title: 3. Create a PostgreSQL database
docs.text:
Use any PostgreSQL provider (Neon, Supabase, local). Copy the connection string — it looks like:

postgresql://user:password@host:5432/database_name

docs.title: 4. Set up the Discord bot
docs.text:
1. Go to the Discord Developer Portal
  2. Create a new application and add a bot
  3. Under Bot settings, enable:
  4. Server Members Intent
  5. Message Content Intent
  6. Copy the bot token
  7. Invite the bot to your server with permissions: Send Messages, Embed Links, Read Messages/History

docs.title: 5. Create a .env file
docs.text:
DISCORD_TOKEN=your_bot_token_here
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://user:password@host:5432/database_name
LOG_LEVEL=INFO
USE_UMAMOE_API=true
UMAMOE_API_KEY=uma_k_your_key_here

Set USE_UMAMOE_API=false to use ChronoGenesis scraping instead of Uma.moe API.

UMAMOE_API_KEY is required for all Uma.moe API endpoints. Pass it via the X-API-Key request header. Contact the uma.moe admin to obtain a key.

docs.title: 6. Run the bot
docs.text:
python main.py

On first run, the bot automatically creates all database tables, syncs slash commands, and starts the scheduler.

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
Once the bot is running, do this to get started:

1. Add your club

/add_club club_name:YourClub scrape_url:... circle_id:860280110

2. Set up channels

/set_report_channel club:YourClub channel:#daily-reports
/set_alert_channel club:YourClub channel:#mod-alerts

3. Test it

/force_check club:YourClub

4. (Optional) Post the monthly info board

/post_monthly_info club:YourClub channel:#info

docs.title: Finding Your Circle ID
docs.text:
If you want to use the Uma.moe API (recommended):

  1. Go to uma.moe/circles
  2. Search for your club
  3. Copy the numeric ID from the URL — e.g. https://uma.moe/circles/860280110 → use 860280110

docs.title: Docker
docs.text: docker-compose up -d --build

docs.title: Railway / Render / Fly.io
docs.text:
Create a Procfile:

worker: python main.py

docs.title: Linux (systemd)
docs.text:
sudo nano /etc/systemd/system/umacore.service
sudo systemctl enable umacore
sudo systemctl start umacore

docs.title: Database Tables
docs.text:
All tables are created automatically on first run:

Table Purpose clubs Club configurations and settings members Club member data quota_history Daily quota tracking per member quota_requirements Quota change history bombs Active bomb warnings user_links Discord ID to trainer mappings bot_settings Monthly info board locations club_rank_history Club ranking over time

docs.title: Terms of Service
docs.text:
Last updated: April 1, 2026

By inviting UmaCore to your Discord server or using any of its commands, you agree to these Terms of Service.

docs.title: 1. Description of Service
docs.text: UmaCore is a Discord bot that tracks Umamusume: Pretty Derby club member quotas, manages warning systems, and generates performance reports for Discord servers.

docs.title: 2. Eligibility
docs.text: You must comply with Discord's Terms of Service and be of the minimum age required in your country to use Discord in order to use UmaCore.

docs.title: 3. Acceptable Use
docs.text:
You agree not to:

  • Use UmaCore to harass, abuse, or harm other users
  • Attempt to exploit, spam, or otherwise abuse the bot
  • Use the bot for any unlawful purpose

Server administrators are responsible for ensuring their members use UmaCore appropriately within their servers.

docs.title: 4. Data
docs.text: UmaCore stores server-specific configuration and member data necessary to provide its services (see our Privacy Policy for details). By using UmaCore you consent to this data being stored and processed.

docs.title: 5. Availability
docs.text: UmaCore is provided on a best-effort basis. We do not guarantee uptime, and the service may be modified, suspended, or discontinued at any time without notice.

docs.title: 6. Disclaimer of Warranties
docs.text: UmaCore is provided "as is" without any warranty of any kind, express or implied. We are not responsible for any damages or losses resulting from your use of the bot.

docs.title: 7. Changes to Terms
docs.text: We reserve the right to update these Terms at any time. Continued use of UmaCore after changes are posted constitutes acceptance of the new Terms.

docs.title: 8. Contact
docs.text: For questions or concerns, open an issue on GitHub or contact the developer via Discord.

docs.title: Commands
docs.text: All commands are Discord slash commands (/).

docs.title: Categories
docs.text:
• Club Management — Add, edit, remove, and list clubs
  • Quota Management — Set quotas, view history, force checks
  • Channel Settings — Configure report and alert channels
  • Member Management — Add, activate, deactivate members
  • User Commands — Self-service commands for members
  • Charts & Stats — Progress charts and bot statistics

docs.title: Quick Reference
docs.text: Command Who Description /add_club Admin Register a new club /remove_club Admin Delete a club /edit_club Admin Modify club settings /list_clubs Admin View all clubs in server /activate_club Admin Reactivate a deactivated club /quota Admin Set daily quota for a club /quota_history Admin View quota changes this month /delete_quota Admin Remove a specific quota entry /force_check Admin Manually trigger daily check /recalculate Admin Recalculate bombs without clearing data /reset_month Admin Manually trigger monthly reset /set_report_channel Admin Set daily report channel /set_alert_channel Admin Set alert channel /channel_settings Admin View channel config /post_monthly_info Admin Post monthly info board /update_monthly_info Admin Refresh monthly info board /add_member Admin Manually add a member /deactivate_member Admin Deactivate a member /activate_member Admin Reactivate a member /bomb_status Admin View active bombs /link_trainer Member Link Discord to trainer name /unlink Member Remove trainer link /my_status Member View your own quota status /member_status Member View any member's status /notification_settings Member Manage DM preferences /progress_chart Anyone Fan progression chart this month /previous_month Anyone Last month's final fan stats /stats Author Bot-wide statistics

docs.title: Channel Settings
docs.text: These commands are restricted to server admins.

docs.title: /set_report_channel
docs.text:
Set the channel where daily quota reports are posted.

Parameter Required Description channel Yes Discord text channel club Yes Target club

docs.title: /set_alert_channel
docs.text:
Set the channel where alerts (bombs, kicks) are posted.

Parameter Required Description channel Yes Discord text channel club Yes Target club

docs.title: /channel_settings
docs.text:
View the current report and alert channel configuration for a club.

Parameter Required Description club Yes Target club

docs.title: /post_monthly_info
docs.text:
Post the monthly info board to a channel. This embed automatically updates whenever quota changes are made.

Parameter Required Description club Yes Target club channel No Channel to post in (defaults to report channel)

docs.title: /update_monthly_info
docs.text:
Manually refresh the monthly info board embed.

Parameter Required Description club Yes Target club

docs.title: /progress_chart
docs.text:
Generate a cumulative fan progression chart for all members in a club this month.

Parameter Required Description club Yes Target club

The chart shows one line per active member with dates on the X-axis and cumulative fans on the Y-axis. Members who joined mid-month will only have data from their join date onward.

Data sources (in priority order): 1. Uma.moe API — full month history (requires circle_id) 2. Database fallback for ChronoGenesis clubs

On day 1 of the month, the chart falls back to previous month data since the current month hasn't populated yet.

docs.title: /previous_month
docs.text:
View last month's final fan totals for all members, ranked by performance.

Parameter Required Description club Yes Target club

Shows each member's total fans earned last month. Members who joined mid-month are indicated.

docs.title: /stats
docs.text:
View bot-wide statistics including total clubs, members, active bombs, and uptime.

Restricted to the bot author only.

No parameters.

docs.title: Club Management
docs.text: These commands are restricted to server admins.

docs.title: /add_club
docs.text:
Register a new club to track.

Parameter Required Description club_name Yes Name of the club scrape_url Yes ChronoGenesis URL for the club circle_id No Numeric ID from uma.moe (recommended) daily_quota No Fan goal per period (default: 1,000,000) quota_period No daily, weekly, or biweekly (default: daily) timezone No IANA timezone (default: Europe/Amsterdam) scrape_time No Time to run daily check in HH:MM (default: 16:00)

Example:

/add_club club_name:MyClub scrape_url:https://... circle_id:860280110 daily_quota:1000000

docs.title: /remove_club
docs.text:
Permanently delete a club and all its data. Asks for confirmation before proceeding.

Parameter Required Description club Yes Club to remove

docs.title: /activate_club
docs.text:
Reactivate a previously deactivated club.

Parameter Required Description club Yes Club to reactivate

docs.title: /list_clubs
docs.text:
View all clubs registered in this server, including their status and basic settings.

No parameters.

docs.title: /edit_club
docs.text:
Modify settings for an existing club.

Parameter Required Description club Yes Club to edit circle_id No Update uma.moe circle ID daily_quota No Update quota amount quota_period No Update quota period (daily, weekly, biweekly) scrape_time No Update daily check time (HH:MM) timezone No Update timezone bomb_trigger_days No Days behind before bomb activates (default: 3) bomb_countdown_days No Days to get back on track (default: 7) bombs_enabled No Enable or disable bomb system (true/false)

Only include the parameters you want to change.

docs.title: Member Management
docs.text: These commands are restricted to server admins.

docs.title: /add_member
docs.text:
Manually add a member to a club. The bot auto-detects members from scrape data, but this lets you add someone early or manually.

Parameter Required Description trainer_name Yes In-game trainer name join_date Yes Date they joined (YYYY-MM-DD) club Yes Target club trainer_id No Numeric trainer ID (if known)

docs.title: /deactivate_member
docs.text:
Deactivate a member. They won't be auto-reactivated even if they appear in future scrapes.

Parameter Required Description trainer_name Yes Trainer name to deactivate club Yes Target club

docs.title: /activate_member
docs.text:
Reactivate a previously deactivated member.

Parameter Required Description trainer_name Yes Trainer name to reactivate club Yes Target club

docs.title: /bomb_status
docs.text:
View all currently active bombs for a club, including how many days remain on each.

Parameter Required Description club Yes Target club

docs.title: Quota Management
docs.text: These commands are restricted to server admins.

docs.title: /quota
docs.text:
Set the daily quota requirement for a club. Takes effect from today onwards.

Parameter Required Description amount Yes Fan goal (integer) club Yes Target club

Example:

/quota amount:1500000 club:MyClub

docs.title: /quota_history
docs.text:
View all quota changes made this month for a club.

Parameter Required Description club Yes Target club

docs.title: /delete_quota
docs.text:
Remove a specific quota entry from history. Useful for correcting mistakes.

Parameter Required Description club Yes Target club date Yes Date of the entry (YYYY-MM-DD) amount Yes Quota amount to remove

docs.title: /force_check
docs.text:
Manually trigger a daily check immediately. Scrapes current data and posts a report to the report channel.

Parameter Required Description club Yes Club to check

docs.title: /recalculate
docs.text:
Recalculate days-behind counts and bomb statuses based on existing data, without clearing anything.

Parameter Required Description club Yes Club to recalculate

docs.title: /reset_month
docs.text:
Manually trigger a monthly reset. Clears quota history, bombs, and tracking data for the new month.

Parameter Required Description club No Specific club to reset (omit to reset all) skip_confirmation No Skip the confirmation prompt

The bot normally handles monthly resets automatically when it detects fan counts have dropped significantly. Only use this if something went wrong.

docs.title: User Commands
docs.text: These commands are available to all members.

docs.title: /link_trainer
docs.text:
Link your Discord account to your in-game trainer name. Required for DM notifications and /my_status.

Parameter Required Description trainer_name Yes Your in-game trainer name club Yes Your club

docs.title: /unlink
docs.text:
Remove your trainer link.

No parameters.

docs.title: /my_status
docs.text:
View your own current quota status, including fans today, deficit/surplus, and bomb status. Requires being linked via /link_trainer.

No parameters.

docs.title: /member_status
docs.text:
View the quota status of any member by name.

Parameter Required Description trainer_name Yes Trainer name to look up club Yes Their club

docs.title: /notification_settings
docs.text:
Manage which DM notifications you receive.

Parameter Required Description bomb_warnings No Receive DMs when you get a bomb (true/false) deficit_alerts No Receive DMs when you fall behind quota (true/false)
